articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

article.headline: Guirassy double guides Stuttgart 3-2 past Wolfsburg
article.description: VfB Stuttgart top scorer Serhou Guirassy struck twice to guide them to a 3-2 victory at VfL Wolfsburg on Saturday, tightening their hold on third spot in the Bundesliga standings and closing in on Bayern Munich in second.
article.linkText: Guirassy double guides Stuttgart 3-2 past Wolfsburg

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (VfL Wolfsburg vs. VfB Stuttgart - Game Highlights) 2024/03/02 ESHEET

article.story: BERLIN, March 2 (Reuters) - VfB Stuttgart top scorer Serhou Guirassy struck twice to guide them to a 3-2 victory at VfL Wolfsburg on Saturday, tightening their hold on third spot in the Bundesliga standings and closing in on Bayern Munich in second. Guirassy, who now has 20 league goals this season, put the visitors in front with a glancing header in the 14th minute. The Wolves equalised five minutes after halftime with Joakim Maehle beating the offside line and finding enough space in the box to curl a shot past goalkeeper Alexander Nuebel. Maehle, however, was judged to have brought down Enzo Millot in the box and Guirassy stepped up to convert the spot kick for a renewed lead in the 54th minute. Josha Vagnoman gave them a two-goal cushion in the 77th before Wolfsburg pulled a goal back with a Lukas Nmecha effort six minutes later. Stuttgart are third on 50 points, four behind Bayern, who stumbled to a 2-2 draw at Freiburg on Friday. Borussia Dortmund, in fourth, are six points behind on 44. The top four teams qualify for next season's Champions League. (Reporting by Karolos Grohmann Editing by Christian Radnedge)

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Maximilian Arnold (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  VfL Wolfsburg 0, VfB Stuttgart 1. Serhou Guirassy (VfB Stuttgart) header from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Maximilian Mittelstädt with a cross.
Ridle Baku (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
First Half ends, VfL Wolfsburg 0, VfB Stuttgart 1.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Tiago Tomás replaces Václav Cerny.
Goal!  VfL Wolfsburg 1, VfB Stuttgart 1. Joakim Mæhle (VfL Wolfsburg) right footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Lovro Majer.
Joakim Mæhle (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  VfL Wolfsburg 1, VfB Stuttgart 2. Serhou Guirassy (VfB Stuttgart) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom right corner.
Waldemar Anton (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Amin Sarr replaces Lovro Majer.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Jonas Wind replaces Kevin Behrens.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Jamie Leweling replaces Chris Führich.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Deniz Undav replaces Enzo Millot.
Goal!  VfL Wolfsburg 1, VfB Stuttgart 3. Josha Vagnoman (VfB Stuttgart) left footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Jamie Leweling.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Aster Vranckx replaces Yannick Gerhardt.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Lukas Nmecha replaces Kevin Paredes.
Goal!  VfL Wolfsburg 2, VfB Stuttgart 3. Lukas Nmecha (VfL Wolfsburg) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner    following a set piece situation.
Atakan Karazor (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Jeong Woo-Yeong replaces Serhou Guirassy.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Silas Katompa Mvumpa replaces Maximilian Mittelstädt.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Mahmoud Dahoud replaces Angelo Stiller.
Second Half ends, VfL Wolfsburg 2, VfB Stuttgart 3.
